text: Na Rebeka ɔmɛni ni, ɔdi udinkɔ. Anwuɔsu ni, Isak ɔ́kpali Katɔti na nwuna lili na nwuna tawɔ tawɔ ɔsu. Na Unwoti Katɔti lɛnɔ únu-nwu bɔkpali na ɔ́ya na ulekudi Rebeka úbada kaƒɔ.
translated: And Rebekah heard it, and she turned away. Isaac loved Esau because he ate of his hunting. And Rebekah loved Jacob. And Isaac said to Esau, "See, I am old; I do not know the day of my death. Now then, take your gear, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me. And prepare for me the kind of food I like, and bring it to me that I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die."

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Nsu Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́kpali ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Wɛ na akya?”
translated: What are you doing here?

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Mmɔ na Abraham ɔ́su nwuna ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Oowo! Tasɔku mina ubi tesienku mina kaduli bekyuli ɔma.”
translated: The father of Abraham said to his children, “Look! I am old; I do not have the strength to go with you.”

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Na uwi ɔmɛni kemmo ni, ɔnana ku uleku ɔmmɔ beli kasɔngya ɔni na kɔƒa kɔdi kulekpo-ma.
translated: When the elders gather, they discuss issues concerning the chieftaincy and the stool and then they go and deliberate on them.

translated: And Reu lived two hundred and seven years, and begat Peleg.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Katɔti údie ukyuli na ɔ́lɛ-nwu na nwuna udu kenye. Údie-ma, na badi ɔnana ku uleku.
translated: The chief elder of Katɔti's town died and they went to wail and cry at the house yesterday. The town people, they will be there till the next day (tomorrow).

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: Na úduo Unwoti ka údie ukyuli ɔ́kya kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na nwuna kayi kénu ɔlɛ geen.
translated: The chief and Unwoti went to remove thatch from the house that day. And they didn't return until it was dark.

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su-nwu lɛnɔ ɔnini, “Ada kaƒɔ ɔƒaani, na adubo ɔnanabi bɔwɔ. Na adubo-nwu lɛnyi bɔsia anii Ismael. Ɔsika Unwoti únu ƒula kɔnyana kɔni na eewulo.
translated: And he went and told his father, “The day you gave me food, I was very happy. And my happiness was greater than that of Ismael. God has blessed your child so that he is very well."

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Na uwi ɔdi kama ni, Lea úbawɔ ulekubi úte Yakob na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Dina.
translated: When she heard this, Leah took Jacob and gave him to her maid Zilpah as a concubine, to have children for her.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.

translated: He was still struggling when Jacob gave the place the name Peniel, saying, “I have seen God face to face, and yet my life has been saved.”
